description: May, her recovery monitored by Fitz and Radcliffe, tries to push for more freedom, while AIDA and Radcliffe discuss the ethics and necessity of lying. May praises AIDA's no-nonsense attitude, unaware of her true nature. Radcliffe later instructs AIDA that sometimes lies are required to protect lives, especially while her existence is kept from S.H.I.E.L.D.
location: Dr. Holden Radcliffe's house (New York / Radcliffe lab)

transcript.line: So, you see, to cease the hormonal cascade produced by the adrenal medulla and prevent further physical degradation...
Oh, that's the only thing I understand.
The point is, even though you may be feeling better, you suffered some very real, very severe brain trauma.
That said, we're gonna have to keep you just a wee bit longer for further testing.
Just to track your improvements before we release you.
Agent May says you can shove all your tests up your ass.
You speak Chinese? Where are you from?
Well, most of me is Chinese.

Canadian. She's a Ca... She's Canadian, Ontario. Yeah, yeah, big Chinese population there. She did move around a lot as a kid, ‘cause she's an army brat. Oh. Um, AIDA, if you could go and ready the framework for one last scan, please?
You've been hiding AIDA from me. It's too bad. I like her. No nonsense, just business. Very impressive.
Yeah. Yeah, they don't make them like AIDA, do they?
